disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain in the toes and foot is now constant at rest and wakes the patient from sleep, having previously only occurred on walking
trigger_question_examples: When does the pain come on — only when walking, or also at rest?
Does the pain wake you at night?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It used to only hurt when I walked, but now it never stops — it's there even when I'm just lying in bed and it wakes me up in agony.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The distal part of the second toe is blackened, cold and looks dead — established tissue loss
trigger_question_examples: What does the toe look like?
Has the colour of the toe changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The end of my second toe has gone completely black and it feels cold and dead — it wasn't like this a couple of days ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Heavy long-standing tobacco use — approximately 30 cigarettes per day for 15 years
trigger_question_examples: Do you smoke, and how much?
How long have you been smoking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I smoke — about 30 a day, and I've been on that for around 15 years.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Months of cramping arch/foot pain brought on by walking and relieved by rest, preceding the current rest pain
trigger_question_examples: Did you have any foot or leg pain before this, when walking?
How far could you walk before the pain started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: For about six months I've had a cramp in the arch of my foot when I walk, and it would go away when I stopped and rested.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent tender red cord-like lesions on the lower legs that come and go (migratory superficial thrombophlebitis)
trigger_question_examples: Have you ever had tender red streaks or cords on your legs?
Any painful lumps in the veins of your legs before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, yeah — I've had tender red cord-like streaks on my lower legs on and off for a while, they come and go.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Self-medicating heavily with over-the-counter NSAIDs with no relief of the pain
trigger_question_examples: Have you taken anything for the pain?
Have any painkillers helped at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been taking a lot of the anti-inflammatory tablets from the chemist, more than the box says, but they do nothing for it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no spreading redness up the leg, no feeling systemically unwell at present
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever or feel unwell in yourself?
Is the redness spreading up your foot or leg?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't have a fever and the redness isn't spreading up my leg — I just feel it's the toe and the pain.
